verdict.headline: New notification backend feature added; verify security of self-hosted backend
verdict.release_summary: The v1.15.11_2.14.0 release introduces a significant feature: support for a self-hosted notification backend, allowing users to configure their own backend for push notifications (commit 9d583c04 by @nickclyde). This change modifies how notifications are handled and could expose user data to security risks if the backend is not properly secured. Additionally, a new Reddit API Secret field has been added for forwarding credentials to the backend. The release also includes various performance improvements and a project restructure, but no new external hosts or analytics SDKs were introduced.
top_changes_to_review: 9d583c04 by @nickclyde: 'Restore push notification functionality from apollo-backend' — verify the security of the self-hosted backend and how user data is handled.
b39f6d74 by @JeffreyCA: 'Perf/reliability improvements' — UI-only fixes, low risk.

anomalies.what: Introduction of a self-hosted notification backend
anomalies.why_it_matters: This change could expose user data to security risks if the backend is not properly secured.

decision_inputs: Check the security measures in place for the self-hosted backend at https://github.com/nickclyde/apollo-backend
Review the commit 9d583c04 for details on the notification backend implementation
verdict.reasoning: The release notes indicate the addition of a self-hosted notification backend, which aligns with the changes made in the code. The presence of a new committer and the introduction of a potentially risky feature warrant a review, but there are no indications of malicious intent.

reviews.title: Restore push notification functionality from apollo-backend
reviews.author: nickclyde
reviews.summary: This commit restores push notification functionality by adding support for a self-hosted notification backend. It modifies how requests are routed to legacy hosts and introduces a new module for handling notifications.
reviews.security_implications: The introduction of a custom notification backend could expose the application to security risks if the backend is not properly secured.
reviews.privacy_implications: User data may be sent to a self-hosted backend, which could have implications for data privacy depending on the backend's security and data handling practices.

reviews.title: Perf/reliability improvements
reviews.author: JeffreyCA
reviews.summary: This commit focuses on performance and reliability improvements across various UI components, enhancing the overall user experience.

reviews.title: Restructure project
reviews.author: JeffreyCA
reviews.summary: This commit restructures the project, adding a LICENSE file and simplifying documentation. It primarily focuses on organization rather than functional changes.
